headline: One platform. Full testing pipeline. Zero humans.
body: Testsprite is an autonomous testing platform that does not just run your tests. It builds the test plan, writes the code, executes everything, and debugs failures. Start to finish, no human in the loop.

headline: 30-40% of dev time becomes 5%
body: The average engineering team spends 30-40% of total development time on testing. Testsprite cuts that to under 5% by handling the entire testing pipeline autonomously. That is not an improvement. It is a restructure of how engineering time gets allocated.

headline: 200-400 test cases vs your team's 40-60
body: A single Testsprite run generates 200-400 test cases for a mid-size application. Manual QA teams typically produce 40-60 in the same timeframe. The coverage gap is not marginal. It is 5-8x.

headline: Debug time: 3 hours down to 15 minutes
body: Debugging test failures used to take 2-3 hours per failure. Testsprite analyzes the failure, traces the root cause, and suggests the fix. Average resolution time drops to under 15 minutes.

headline: The winners are not the biggest QA teams
body: The companies adopting autonomous testing first are not the ones with the biggest QA teams. They are the ones that realized QA as a manual discipline was already broken. Testsprite just made the fix obvious.

headline: If your testing pipeline still needs humans, you are already behind. Follow for more AI engineering tools.
